book.title_en: A Glass Bridge Between Two Worlds
book.title_ja: ビーズの魔法：ムラノと京都をつなぐガラスの橋
book.author: Maurizio Lotter

ritual_types.furnace_ritual: body enters thermal field; technique becomes physiological adaptation
ritual_types.apprenticeship: knowledge transmitted through spatial co-presence with master
ritual_types.bilateral_making: same technique displaced into a second cultural field
ritual_types.gift_of_glass: compressed transmission of entire system through object transfer
ritual_types.slow_observation: decompression of meaning through temporal attention

editorial_principle: The ritual system does not describe craft: it transmits embodied thresholds. Each gesture is a passage between Murano and Kyoto, where technique becomes environment rather than skill. The bead is not product but residue of transition.
editorial_principle_ja: この儀式体系は技法を説明しない。身体化された閾を伝達する。ムラノと京都のあいだで、技術は技能ではなく環境になる。ビーズは製品ではなく遷移の残余である。

rituals.label: lavoro_alla_fornace
rituals.basho: The furnace is a thermal field that replaces environment: body and fire form a single operative system in which glass exists only in transformation.
rituals.kokyu: Respiration aligns with heat cycles; gesture is synchronized with viscosity thresholds of molten matter.
rituals.yūgen: Every intervention is irreversible: once cooled, transformation becomes historical trace, not reversible process.

non_integrable_residue.description: Thermal knowledge cannot be abstracted from bodily exposure.

affects.ritual-02: establishes initiation condition for apprenticeship field

rituals.textual_anchor_en: Feel the heat of the flames, the joy of shaping molten glass.
rituals.textual_anchor_ja: 炎の熱を感じ、溶けたガラスを形作る喜びを味わう。

rituals.label: apprendistato_murano
rituals.basho: Apprenticeship is spatial absorption into the master's operational field; learning occurs through proximity, not instruction.
rituals.kokyu: Repetition compresses time: gesture becomes memory through failure cycles.
rituals.yūgen: Early entry into furnace culture removes separation between learning and survival of technique.

non_integrable_residue.description: Technique cannot be separated from the historical body that acquired it.

affects.ritual-03: enables displacement of technique into Kyoto field

rituals.textual_anchor_en: From an apprenticeship in Murano as a child to the challenges of creating unique, one-of-a-kind works.
rituals.textual_anchor_ja: 幼少期のムラノでの修業から、唯一無二の作品を生み出す挑戦まで。

rituals.label: fare_a_kyoto
rituals.basho: Kyoto is not relocation but reframing: Venetian technique persists inside a contrasting temporal ecology.
rituals.kokyu: Slow artisanal time modulates the same gestures into different outputs.
rituals.yūgen: Coexistence of two craft ontologies without fusion or resolution.

non_integrable_residue.description: The object remains undecidable between Venetian origin and Kyoto production field.

affects.ritual-04: object becomes transmissible gift rather than product

rituals.textual_anchor_en: a master trained in Venice who had been living and working in Kyoto for over thirty years.
rituals.textual_anchor_ja: ヴェネツィアで修行を積み、30年以上京都で制作を続ける職人。

rituals.label: dono_del_vetro
rituals.basho: The bead functions as condensed system transfer: technique, geography, and memory are embedded in a single object.
rituals.kokyu: Gesture of giving compresses production history into instantaneous transfer.
rituals.yūgen: Understanding of the object is delayed; meaning unfolds after possession.

non_integrable_residue.description: Gift cannot be fully interpreted at moment of reception.

rituals.textual_anchor_en: I met Maurizio Lotter last year in Venice. We talked, and he gave me some of his glass beads.
rituals.textual_anchor_ja: 私は昨年ヴェネツィアでマウリツィオ・ロッテル氏と出会いました。彼は自身のガラスビーズをいくつか私に贈ってくれました。

rituals.label: osservazione_lenta
rituals.basho: Observation is the final activation layer: the object becomes legible only under prolonged attention.
rituals.kokyu: Attention decelerates perception until micro-structures emerge (foil, patterns, inclusions).
rituals.yūgen: Final detail (insect-like motif) destabilizes total perception of the object.

non_integrable_residue.description: Full comprehension is never completed; perception remains open.

rituals.textual_anchor_en: She took the beads, observed them closely, and, selecting one, said they were truly fascinating.
rituals.textual_anchor_ja: 彼女はビーズを手に取りじっくり観察すると、一つを選び、「これは本当に魅力的ね」と言いました。

system_note.narrative_model: five-stage transmission system: furnace → apprenticeship → displacement → gift → delayed perception
system_note.bilingual_structure: EN/JA synchronized fields without duplication of meaning
